Set_01.meaning: A very loud, continuous noise.
Set_01.example: The din in the cafeteria made it hard to think.

Set_01.meaning: Very rude and showing no respect.
Set_01.example: His insolent tone shocked the adults.

Set_01.meaning: Very weak.
Set_01.example: The torch gave only a feeble light.

Set_01.meaning: Nobody; not a single person.
Set_01.example: No one knew the answer to the question.

Set_01.meaning: The inside part of something.
Set_01.example: The interior of the castle was grand.

Set_01.meaning: The outside part of something.
Set_01.example: The exterior of the house was freshly painted.

Set_01.meaning: To finish; to decide something after thinking.
Set_01.example: We can conclude that our experiment was a success.

Set_01.meaning: To stick to something.
Set_01.example: The label will adhere better if the surface is clean.

Set_01.meaning: A holy place connected with a person or event.
Set_01.example: Pilgrims visited the shrine every year.

Set_01.meaning: Doing things at the correct time.
Set_01.example: Try to be punctual for your lessons.

Set_01.meaning: A container with flat sides and a lid; to fight with fists (verb).
Set_01.example: Put your books back in the box, please.

Set_01.meaning: To live in a particular place.
Set_01.example: They reside in a house near the park.

Set_01.meaning: Not happening often; unusual.
Set_01.example: It is rare to see snow in April here.

Set_01.meaning: To allow; an official document giving permission.
Set_01.example: You need a permit to park here.

Set_01.meaning: A place where someone lives.
Set_01.example: The old cottage was a cosy dwelling.

Set_01.meaning: Connected to something or having strong feelings for someone.
Set_01.example: The note was attached to the report.

Set_01.meaning: Clever at hiding what you are doing.
Set_01.example: The sly fox crept towards the chickens.

Set_01.meaning: To bring back to life or make active again.
Set_01.example: A drink of water helped revive him.

Set_01.meaning: Shy or pretending to be shy.
Set_01.example: She gave a coy smile and looked away.

Set_01.meaning: An enemy.
Set_01.example: The knight faced his fiercest foe.

Set_01.meaning: Very small in amount.
Set_01.example: There was scanty evidence to prove the claim.

Set_01.meaning: To jump or fall suddenly; to move quickly downwards.
Set_01.example: She took a deep breath and decided to plunge into the water.

Set_01.meaning: An amount of money saved or set aside for a purpose.
Set_01.example: The school started a fund for new computers.

Set_01.meaning: A serious promise.
Set_01.example: He took an oath to tell the truth.

Set_01.meaning: Happening often.
Set_01.example: He made frequent visits to the library.

Set_01.meaning: To plant seeds in the ground.
Set_01.example: Farmers sow crops in spring.

Set_01.meaning: Help or support.
Set_01.example: The extra lessons were a great aid to her progress.

Set_01.meaning: Something unexpected; to amaze someone.
Set_01.example: The party was a lovely surprise.

Set_01.meaning: To put something into something else.
Set_01.example: Insert your card into the machine.

Set_01.meaning: Whole; complete.
Set_01.example: He read the entire book in one weekend.

Set_02.meaning: Able to move quickly and lightly.
Set_02.example: The nimble dancer leapt across the stage.

Set_02.meaning: A choice you can make.
Set_02.example: You have the option to stay or go.

Set_02.meaning: Very small in amount.
Set_02.example: They survived on a meagre supply of food.

Set_02.meaning: Something small and not very important.
Set_02.example: It is a mere trifle, don’t worry.

Set_02.meaning: A heavy load or a difficult responsibility.
Set_02.example: Caring for a sick relative can be a burden.

Set_02.meaning: To shake slightly; a slight shake.
Set_02.example: His hands began to quiver with cold.

Set_02.meaning: A piece of clothing.
Set_02.example: She folded each garment carefully.

Set_02.meaning: Nothing more than; only.
Set_02.example: It was a mere scratch, not a deep cut.

Set_02.meaning: A substance that gives colour.
Set_02.example: The artist mixed blue pigment with white.

Set_02.meaning: To cause a feeling or reaction in someone.
Set_02.example: The loud noise will arouse the neighbours’ anger.

Set_02.meaning: To express great sadness or regret.
Set_02.example: The poet wrote to lament his lost friend.

Set_02.meaning: Very short and almost rude.
Set_02.example: His curt answer ended the conversation quickly.

Set_02.meaning: Thin; to bend your body or rest it against something.
Set_02.example: He had to lean against the wall for support.

Set_02.meaning: Great mental or physical suffering; to cause such suffering.
Set_02.example: Guilt can be a terrible torment.

Set_02.meaning: An arm or a leg.
Set_02.example: He broke a limb while climbing a tree.

Set_02.meaning: To show or make known.
Set_02.example: The magician would not reveal his secret.

Set_02.meaning: To die, especially in a terrible way.
Set_02.example: Many plants would perish without water.

Set_02.meaning: Able to think normally; sensible.
Set_02.example: No sane person would run into a burning building.

Set_02.meaning: Very difficult and requiring a lot of effort.
Set_02.example: Climbing the hill was an arduous journey.

Set_02.meaning: A measured amount of medicine.
Set_02.example: Take one dose of the medicine after meals.

Set_02.meaning: The quality that things have when we see them as red, blue etc.
Set_02.example: She chose a bright colour for her bedroom walls.

Set_02.meaning: The pointed top of a mountain; the highest level.
Set_02.example: They reached the peak of the mountain at noon.

Set_02.meaning: Connected with a god; very special and respected.
Set_02.example: The temple is a sacred place.

Set_02.meaning: Clothing, especially fine clothes.
Set_02.example: The king wore rich raiment at the ceremony.

Set_02.meaning: Behaving as if you are very important.
Set_02.example: His pompous speech bored the audience.

Set_02.meaning: A position in a group; to put in order.
Set_02.example: He rose to the rank of captain.

Set_02.meaning: Very large in size or amount.
Set_02.example: The desert seemed vast.

Set_02.meaning: Very brave.
Set_02.example: The firefighter’s heroic act saved lives.

Set_02.meaning: To give someone a job; to use something.
Set_02.example: The company will employ ten new workers.

Set_02.meaning: Tidy and carefully arranged.
Set_02.example: Her handwriting is very neat.

Set_03.meaning: Used to refer to people or things already mentioned.
Set_03.example: They went to the park after school.

Set_03.meaning: To gently persuade someone to do something.
Set_03.example: She tried to coax the cat out from under the bed.

Set_03.meaning: Into separate parts; apart.
Set_03.example: The old bridge was torn asunder by the flood.

Set_03.meaning: A sign that is believed to show what will happen.
Set_03.example: They saw the black cat as a bad omen.

Set_03.meaning: Worried and nervous about something.
Set_03.example: She felt anxious before the exam.

Set_03.meaning: Birds kept for their meat or eggs.
Set_03.example: They keep poultry such as hens and ducks.

Set_03.meaning: To get something.
Set_03.example: Did you receive my message?

Set_03.meaning: A trick that makes people believe something false.
Set_03.example: The news story turned out to be a hoax.

Set_03.meaning: A small stream or brook.
Set_03.example: The children splashed in the shallow beck.

Set_03.meaning: Wanting to know or learn about something.
Set_03.example: The curious child asked many questions.

Set_03.meaning: Received money for work; past of “pay”.
Set_03.example: She was paid for mowing the lawn.

Set_03.meaning: To make someone believe something that is not true.
Set_03.example: He tried to deceive his parents about his homework.

Set_03.meaning: To think that something is true.
Set_03.example: I believe you did your best in the test.

Set_03.meaning: Not working or being used; lazy.
Set_03.example: The machines lay idle during the holiday.

Set_03.meaning: The first person to explore or do something new.
Set_03.example: She was a pioneer in medical research.

Set_03.meaning: Something bought cheaply; an agreement between two sides.
Set_03.example: The coat was a real bargain in the sale.

Set_03.meaning: A plan of things to do; a TV or radio show.
Set_03.example: I watched a nature programme last night.

Set_03.meaning: Not acting, only letting things happen.
Set_03.example: He took a passive role in the discussion.

Set_03.meaning: Not legal or correct; too sick or weak to move.
Set_03.example: The ticket was invalid after the date.

Set_03.meaning: To say that something is not true.
Set_03.example: He will deny breaking the window.

Set_03.meaning: To move around without a fixed purpose.
Set_03.example: Cows roam freely in the field.

Set_03.meaning: To move forward or make progress.
Set_03.example: The soldiers began to advance towards the town.

Set_03.meaning: The state of not being present.
Set_03.example: His absence from class was noticed by the teacher.

Set_03.meaning: A sailor.
Set_03.example: The old mariner told stories of the sea.

Set_03.meaning: A place where books are kept for people to read or borrow.
Set_03.example: She borrowed three books from the library.

Set_03.meaning: Care taken to avoid danger or mistakes.
Set_03.example: Use caution when crossing the busy road.

Set_03.meaning: Related to the body; real and able to be touched.
Set_03.example: Regular physical exercise keeps you healthy.

Set_03.meaning: To strongly dislike and have no respect for.
Set_03.example: She began to despise his selfish behaviour.

Set_03.meaning: Grassland where animals feed.
Set_03.example: Cows grazed in the green pasture.

Set_03.meaning: Someone you admire and love very much.
Set_03.example: The footballer was his idol.

Set_04.meaning: A feeling that there is no hope.
Set_04.example: She cried in despair after losing the final.

Set_04.meaning: To stop or come to a stop.
Set_04.example: The train will halt at the next station.

Set_04.meaning: A person or animal you spend time with.
Set_04.example: The dog was a loyal companion on his walks.

Set_04.meaning: Very, very much.
Set_04.example: She was extremely pleased with her results.

Set_04.meaning: Someone who betrays their country or friends.
Set_04.example: The spy was called a traitor.

Set_04.meaning: Fair treatment according to the law.
Set_04.example: The judge tried to bring justice to the case.

Set_04.meaning: To bring something back to its original state.
Set_04.example: The museum will restore the old painting.

Set_04.meaning: A thin piece of cloth that covers the face or head.
Set_04.example: The bride wore a white veil.

Set_04.meaning: Having many parts; not easy to understand.
Set_04.example: The machine had a complex set of controls.

Set_04.meaning: To control or limit something.
Set_04.example: New rules will curb dangerous driving.

Set_04.meaning: Not strict; mild in punishing.
Set_04.example: The teacher was lenient about late homework.

Set_04.meaning: A group of people living in the same place or sharing interests.
Set_04.example: The community raised money for the new playground.

Set_04.meaning: Complete confusion and lack of order.
Set_04.example: There was chaos in the hall after the alarm rang.

Set_04.meaning: To cause a reaction, especially anger.
Set_04.example: His rude words were meant to provoke her.

Set_04.meaning: A person or group that supports you.
Set_04.example: In the debate, she was glad to have an ally.

Set_04.meaning: Good for your health or character.
Set_04.example: They cooked a wholesome meal.

Set_04.meaning: To stay away from someone or something.
Set_04.example: You should avoid eating too much junk food.

Set_04.meaning: Needed and essential.
Set_04.example: A pen is necessary for this exam.

Set_04.meaning: The greatest amount allowed.
Set_04.example: There is a time limit for this exam.

Set_04.meaning: To steal things by force.
Set_04.example: Pirates used to plunder ships at sea.

Set_04.meaning: Marking the beginning of something important.
Set_04.example: The president gave his inaugural speech.

Set_04.meaning: The usual level or typical amount; not special.
Set_04.example: His score was about average for the class.

Set_04.meaning: Very confused.
Set_04.example: She looked perplexed by the puzzle.

Set_04.meaning: To run away from danger.
Set_04.example: People had to flee the burning building.

Set_04.meaning: Kindness shown to someone who is in your power.
Set_04.example: The king decided to show mercy to the prisoner.

Set_04.meaning: Very ugly or strange in a frightening way.
Set_04.example: The mask had a grotesque and twisted face.

Set_04.meaning: Unable to speak; sometimes used to mean silly.
Set_04.example: The accident left him temporarily dumb.

Set_04.meaning: To decide something firmly; to discover something.
Set_04.example: She is determined to improve her spelling.

Set_04.meaning: Discussed something together and decided.
Set_04.example: The teachers conferred before setting the exam date.

Set_04.meaning: To hang from something; to stop something for a time.
Set_04.example: The match was suspend­ed because of rain.

Set_05.meaning: The part of the body where food goes; belly.
Set_05.example: His stomach hurt after eating too quickly.

Set_05.meaning: Very old and respected.
Set_05.example: They visited a venerable old teacher.

Set_05.meaning: Very easy to see or notice; important.
Set_05.example: The tower is a prominent landmark in the city.

Set_05.meaning: To hide something or keep it secret.
Set_05.example: He tried to conceal the gift behind his back.

Set_05.meaning: A feeling of being thankful.
Set_05.example: He showed gratitude by writing a thank‑you card.

Set_05.meaning: A sudden flash of electricity in the sky during a storm.
Set_05.example: The lightning lit up the whole sky.

Set_05.meaning: A competition or struggle.
Set_05.example: The spelling contest will be held next week.

Set_05.meaning: Ready for use; able to be used or obtained.
Set_05.example: The teacher was available to help after school.

Set_05.meaning: To think that something is good and agree with it.
Set_05.example: His parents approve of his hard work.

Set_05.meaning: Not shiny.
Set_05.example: She chose a matte finish for the paint.

Set_05.meaning: All the plants of a particular area.
Set_05.example: The island’s flora includes many rare flowers.

Set_05.meaning: A serious argument or disagreement.
Set_05.example: The neighbours had a dispute about the fence.

Set_05.meaning: Permission for something to happen.
Set_05.example: You need your parents’ consent to go on the trip.

Set_05.meaning: Relating to the countryside.
Set_05.example: They moved from a rural village to a city.

Set_05.meaning: Right, without mistakes; to fix an error.
Set_05.example: Please correct any spelling mistakes in your work.

Set_05.meaning: To stop happening or stop doing something.
Set_05.example: The noise did not cease until midnight.

Set_05.meaning: To stop something like a protest or fear.
Set_05.example: The teacher tried to quell the noisy class.

Set_05.meaning: An unfair opinion about someone before knowing them.
Set_05.example: Prejudice can lead to injustice.

Set_05.meaning: To help something grow or become successful; to give someone a higher job.
Set_05.example: The leaflet aims to promote healthy eating.

Set_05.meaning: Wide; covering a large area.
Set_05.example: They walked along the broad road into town.

Set_05.meaning: To force someone to leave a place.
Set_05.example: The school may expel pupils who cheat.

Set_05.meaning: Burnt and blackened on the surface.
Set_05.example: The toast was so charred it was inedible.

Set_05.meaning: Very stubborn and unwilling to change.
Set_05.example: The obstinate child refused to apologise.

Set_05.meaning: To stop someone from speaking or doing something.
Set_05.example: Please do not interrupt while I am talking.

Set_05.meaning: Making little or no noise.
Set_05.example: Please be quiet in the library.

Set_05.meaning: To put up or build something; standing straight.
Set_05.example: They will erect a new fence around the field.

Set_05.meaning: To take something quickly and firmly.
Set_05.example: The police will seize stolen goods.

Set_05.meaning: To explain the meaning of something.
Set_05.example: Can you interpret this graph for the class?

Set_05.meaning: Able to produce many plants or babies.
Set_05.example: The fertile soil produced a big harvest.

Set_05.meaning: Very great age; ancient times.
Set_05.example: The ruins are treasures from antiquity.

Set_06.meaning: The reason for doing something.
Set_06.example: The purpose of homework is to help you practise.

Set_06.meaning: Extremely poor, without the basic things you need.
Set_06.example: The storm left many families destitute.

Set_06.meaning: Having too high an opinion of yourself.
Set_06.example: His conceited remarks annoyed his classmates.

Set_06.meaning: Your name written in your own way.
Set_06.example: Put your signature at the bottom of the page.

Set_06.meaning: Extreme tiredness.
Set_06.example: After the race, he felt a deep fatigue.

Set_06.meaning: To get something, especially with effort.
Set_06.example: They managed to procure tickets for the show.

Set_06.meaning: Words with the same ending sound.
Set_06.example: “Cat” and “hat” are a simple rhyme.

Set_06.meaning: A vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pedalling.
Set_06.example: She rides her bicycle to school every day.

Set_06.meaning: To grow plants; to develop a skill or relationship.
Set_06.example: Farmers cultivate crops in the fields.

Set_06.meaning: A special advantage or right.
Set_06.example: It is a privilege to attend this school.

Set_06.meaning: A person who is trying to get a job or elected to a position.
Set_06.example: Each candidate gave a short speech.

Set_06.meaning: To take something as your own (a child, an idea, a way).
Set_06.example: The school will adopt a new homework policy.

Set_06.meaning: The natural home of an animal or plant.
Set_06.example: The forest is the natural habitat of many birds.

Set_06.meaning: Quick to notice things; ready to act.
Set_06.example: The guard stayed alert throughout the night.

Set_06.meaning: To happen.
Set_06.example: The accident may occur again if we’re careless.

Set_06.meaning: A serious effort or attempt.
Set_06.example: Passing the exam will require hard endeavour.

Set_06.meaning: To grow or change into something better or more advanced.
Set_06.example: Children develop new skills as they practise.

Set_06.meaning: Holy writings of a religion.
Set_06.example: He studied a passage from scripture.

Set_06.meaning: To cry out suddenly, often in surprise.
Set_06.example: “I can’t believe it!” she began to exclaim.

Set_06.meaning: Pulled to become longer or wider.
Set_06.example: The rope was stretched tightly between the poles.

Set_06.meaning: Great danger.
Set_06.example: They were in peril on the icy road.

Set_06.meaning: Smooth and shiny; elegant.
Set_06.example: The cat’s sleek fur shone in the light.

Set_06.meaning: To escape from or avoid something or someone.
Set_06.example: The mouse managed to elude the cat.

Set_06.meaning: Done too quickly without enough thought.
Set_06.example: It was a hasty decision that he soon regretted.

Set_06.meaning: Different from usual and better or more important.
Set_06.example: They planned a special treat for her birthday.

Set_06.meaning: To go out of sight or no longer exist.
Set_06.example: The rabbit seemed to disappear into the bushes.

Set_06.meaning: A place where a dead person is buried; very serious.
Set_06.example: They laid flowers on the grave of their grandfather.

Set_06.meaning: Confused and unable to think clearly.
Set_06.example: She looked bemused by the difficult question.

Set_06.meaning: To give in to something strong like illness or temptation.
Set_06.example: He refused to succumb to pressure.

Set_06.meaning: A place where goods or vehicles are stored.
Set_06.example: The trains wait at the depot overnight.

Set_07.meaning: Completely; to some degree.
Set_07.example: The test was quite easy this time.

Set_07.meaning: A small piece broken off something.
Set_07.example: She found a fragment of glass on the floor.

Set_07.meaning: Extremely good.
Set_07.example: She did an excellent job on her project.

Set_07.meaning: Very tall; noble or proud.
Set_07.example: The eagle nested on a lofty cliff.

Set_07.meaning: To keep someone or something within limits.
Set_07.example: The sick dog was confine­d to its bed.

Set_07.meaning: Clever at planning in a secret or tricky way.
Set_07.example: The cunning fox escaped from the hunters.

Set_07.meaning: Up to the time when.
Set_07.example: Wait here until I return.

Set_07.meaning: To want something very much, especially something that belongs to someone else.
Set_07.example: You must not covet your neighbour’s toys.

Set_07.meaning: A promise that something will happen or be fixed if it breaks.
Set_07.example: The watch comes with a two‑year guarantee.

Set_07.meaning: The least possible amount.
Set_07.example: The minimum age for this club is ten.

Set_07.meaning: To provide with what is needed.
Set_07.example: The school will equip the lab with new computers.

Set_07.meaning: A surface that is higher at one end than the other.
Set_07.example: They skied down a steep slope.

Set_07.meaning: Not too hot or too cold.
Set_07.example: Britain has a temperate climate.

Set_07.meaning: Lasting only a short time; using few words.
Set_07.example: He gave a brief summary of the story.

Set_07.meaning: To fill someone with the desire to do something.
Set_07.example: Her speech will inspire many pupils to try harder.

Set_07.meaning: To try to do something, especially something difficult.
Set_07.example: His first attempt at the puzzle was not successful.

Set_07.meaning: Safe or satisfactory; acceptable.
Set_07.example: After the fall, the nurse checked he was all right.

Set_07.meaning: To give up something valuable for someone or something else.
Set_07.example: Parents often sacrifice their time for their children.

Set_07.meaning: To get involved in something that is not your business.
Set_07.example: Try not to interfere with their argument.

Set_07.meaning: The details of where someone lives; to speak to someone.
Set_07.example: Please write your full address at the top of the page.

Set_07.meaning: Intended; past tense of “mean”.
Set_07.example: The joke was not meant to be hurtful.

Set_07.meaning: To take something out from somewhere.
Set_07.example: The dentist had to extract the bad tooth.

Set_07.meaning: Very nice to look at.
Set_07.example: The view from the hill was beautiful.

Set_07.meaning: Not graceful; causing difficulty or embarrassment.
Set_07.example: There was an awkward silence after his comment.

Set_07.meaning: The most that is possible.
Set_07.example: The maximum score is 100 marks.

Set_07.meaning: An angry discussion or disagreement.
Set_07.example: They had an argument about the rules.

Set_07.meaning: Happening or done at once.
Set_07.example: The doctor said it needed immediate attention.

Set_07.meaning: Always supporting someone or something.
Set_07.example: The dog was loyal to its owner.

Set_07.meaning: To rub food into small pieces; to irritate.
Set_07.example: She will grate the cheese for the pasta.

Set_07.meaning: Very serious and sincere.
Set_07.example: She made an earnest effort to improve.

Set_08.meaning: A person who protects; to watch over and protect.
Set_08.example: A security guard stood outside the bank.

Set_08.meaning: Quiet and not very bright or strong.
Set_08.example: The class was subdued after the test.

Set_08.meaning: An injury to the body; to injure.
Set_08.example: The nurse cleaned the wound carefully.

Set_08.meaning: Very noisy and rough.
Set_08.example: The rowdy crowd disturbed the neighbours.

Set_08.meaning: Not important.
Set_08.example: Don’t argue about such trivial things.

Set_08.meaning: Joining things or people together; a group formed by joining.
Set_08.example: The union voted on the changes.

Set_08.meaning: To think that something is true, especially bad; a person who may have done a crime.
Set_08.example: The police will question the suspect.

Set_08.meaning: Most liked.
Set_08.example: Chocolate is her favourite flavour of ice cream.

Set_08.meaning: In a genuine and honest way.
Set_08.example: He sincerely hoped she would succeed.

Set_08.meaning: To notice or understand something.
Set_08.example: She could perceive a change in his mood.

Set_08.meaning: To leave something or someone behind, often forever.
Set_08.example: The sailors had to abandon the ship during the storm.

Set_08.meaning: Something good or helpful; an advantage.
Set_08.example: Exercise has a benefit for your health.

Set_08.meaning: The island containing England, Scotland and Wales; often used to mean the UK.
Set_08.example: Many tourists visit Britain each year.

Set_08.meaning: Very stiff and not easily bent; not willing to change.
Set_08.example: The metal bar was rigid and strong.

Set_08.meaning: Feeling like you are going to be sick.
Set_08.example: The boat made him feel nauseous.

Set_08.meaning: A large feather; a column of smoke or water.
Set_08.example: A plume of smoke rose from the chimney.

Set_08.meaning: Permission to enter; a statement that something is true.
Set_08.example: Admission to the museum is free for children.

Set_08.meaning: A person or thing that causes trouble.
Set_08.example: The buzzing fly was a real nuisance.

Set_08.meaning: Saying and feeling what you really mean.
Set_08.example: She gave a sincere apology.

Set_08.meaning: Behaving in a morally good way.
Set_08.example: The hero was brave and virtuous.

Set_08.meaning: Not drunk; serious.
Set_08.example: He gave a sober explanation of the facts.

Set_08.meaning: To decide firmly to do something; to solve a problem.
Set_08.example: They hope to resolve the dispute soon.

Set_08.meaning: To choose someone for a position by voting.
Set_08.example: The class will elect a new representative.

Set_08.meaning: Suitable for people to live in.
Set_08.example: After repairs, the house became habitable again.

Set_08.meaning: Time when you are not working.
Set_08.example: She enjoys reading in her leisure hours.

Set_08.meaning: More than enough; plenty.
Set_08.example: There was ample time to finish the test.

Set_08.meaning: To make someone feel shy, foolish or uncomfortable.
Set_08.example: His clumsy remark seemed to embarrass her.

Set_08.meaning: Too quiet to be heard.
Set_08.example: His whisper was almost inaudible.

Set_08.meaning: Famous or well known for something.
Set_08.example: He is a noted writer of children’s books.

Set_08.meaning: To copy the way someone acts or speaks.
Set_08.example: Children often imitate their parents.

Set_09.meaning: Moving in a violent or uncontrolled way; troubled.
Set_09.example: The plane shook in the turbulent air.

Set_09.meaning: A smooth, shiny fabric.
Set_09.example: The dress was made of blue satin.

Set_09.meaning: To give way; to produce something.
Set_09.example: The field will yield a good crop.

Set_09.meaning: Willing to accept new ideas; generous.
Set_09.example: Her parents have quite liberal views.

Set_09.meaning: To be made up of something.
Set_09.example: The team will consist of ten players.

Set_09.meaning: To bend your body forwards; a small raised area at a door.
Set_09.example: You have to stoop to go through the low door.

Set_09.meaning: Very confused.

Set_09.meaning: Using very few words and seeming unfriendly.

Set_09.meaning: The place where someone lives.

Set_09.meaning: A person who serves in an army.

Set_09.meaning: To begin or start.

Set_09.meaning: Knocked down or destroyed.

Set_09.meaning: To keep something for later use; to book.

Set_09.meaning: To hug; to accept an idea willingly.

Set_09.meaning: The end of something; a final decision.

Set_09.meaning: Very strong or great.

Set_09.meaning: Moving quickly in a way that is out of control.

Set_09.meaning: A long time with very little or no rain.

Set_09.meaning: Very tired.

Set_09.meaning: Very strange.

Set_09.meaning: Clothes that all members of a group wear; always the same.

Set_09.meaning: Very dangerous.

Set_09.meaning: A line of people waiting.

Set_10.meaning: To stay somewhere temporarily; a small house.

Set_10.meaning: A smell, especially an unpleasant one.

Set_10.meaning: A place where you can buy and eat a meal.

Set_10.meaning: Strange or unusual.

Set_10.meaning: A spiral shape like a twisted coil.

Set_10.meaning: Very serious and strict.

Set_10.meaning: To put fully into a liquid; to become very involved in something.

Set_10.meaning: A group of ships or vehicles.

Set_10.meaning: A business; also, being with other people.

Set_10.meaning: To change something into a different form; to change beliefs.

Set_10.meaning: A time when you do not go to work or school; a trip away.

Set_10.meaning: To get or obtain something.

Set_10.meaning: To say something clearly and officially.

Set_10.meaning: To question someone after an event to get information.

Set_10.meaning: A person who behaves badly but sometimes amusingly.

Set_10.meaning: A secret trick used to achieve something.

Set_10.meaning: To treat people cruelly and unfairly.

Set_10.meaning: The number of points; to gain points.

Set_10.meaning: Not usual or traditional.

Set_10.meaning: Having the result you wanted; doing well.

Set_10.meaning: Causing death.

Set_10.meaning: The sad feeling of being alone.

Set_10.meaning: Knowing about something or someone, but not very well.

Set_10.meaning: To make something seem bigger, better or worse than it really is.

Set_10.meaning: To officially begin or open something.

Set_10.meaning: Not able to be touched; only felt or understood.

Set_11.meaning: As much as you need.

Set_11.meaning: Empty and not being used.

Set_11.meaning: Done on purpose; to think about something carefully.

Set_11.meaning: To live in a place.

Set_11.meaning: Friendly and easy to talk to.

Set_11.meaning: Made weaker by adding water or another liquid.

Set_11.meaning: To give something in; to accept someone else’s control.

Set_11.meaning: Very bright, colourful or showy.

Set_11.meaning: To give or help to make something happen.

Set_11.meaning: A person who grows and sells plants.

Set_11.meaning: A single person or thing; separate from others.

Set_11.meaning: A piece of writing in a newspaper or magazine; an object.

Set_11.meaning: Not wasting money or resources.

Set_11.meaning: In the space or time separating two things.

Set_11.meaning: Done quickly; to encourage someone to speak or act.

Set_11.meaning: A project that needs courage or effort; a business.

Set_11.meaning: Happy with what you have; the things inside something.

Set_11.meaning: Something given as a gift; existing now; to show.

Set_11.meaning: Having an empty space inside; not solid.

Set_11.meaning: Safe and well protected; to make something safe.

Set_12.meaning: Related to God; extremely good or beautiful.

Set_12.meaning: Totally; in every way.

Set_12.meaning: Pressure or worry; to give special importance.

Set_12.meaning: Old and in very bad condition.

Set_12.meaning: To become weaker or fewer; to politely say no.

Set_12.meaning: In a way that shows great need, worry, or desire.

Set_13.meaning: To come out or appear.

Set_14.meaning: Relating to the home or family; not wild.

Set_15.meaning: Working very hard and carefully.

Set_15.meaning: A great difference between things.

Set_16.meaning: A place where dead people are buried.

Set_16.meaning: The beginning or where something comes from.

Set_16.meaning: A set of connected parts working together.

Set_16.meaning: Very silly or unreasonable.

Set_16.meaning: The way someone or something looks.

Set_16.meaning: Not willing to change your mind.

Set_16.meaning: A ship or large boat; a container.

Set_17.meaning: To send someone away, often as a punishment.

Set_17.meaning: Not safe or certain; likely to fall.
Set_17.example: The ladder looked very precarious.

Set_17.meaning: A state of untidiness or confusion.
Set_17.example: The room was in complete disarray after the party.

Set_17.meaning: Successful and rich.
Set_17.example: The once poor village is now prosperous.

Set_17.meaning: Not often; rarely.
Set_17.example: We seldom go to the cinema.

Set_17.meaning: Without any mistakes or faults.
Set_17.example: Her performance was almost flawless.

Set_17.meaning: Not as good as something else.
Set_17.example: This copy is inferior to the original.

Set_17.meaning: The base on which something stands; an organisation that supports causes.
Set_17.example: The builders laid a strong foundation for the house.

Set_17.meaning: Liking to be with other people.
Set_17.example: She is so gregarious that she makes friends easily.

Set_17.meaning: A small hole that lets air out.
Set_17.example: The tyre had a puncture after hitting a nail.

Set_17.meaning: Help or support.
Set_17.example: The teacher offered assistance with the difficult task.

Set_17.meaning: Happening all the time; not changing.
Set_17.example: The constant noise made it hard to sleep.

Set_17.meaning: To live in or use a place; to fill time.
Set_17.example: They occupy a small flat near the station.

Set_17.meaning: Very bad and causing great harm.
Set_17.example: The heavy rain was disastrous for the picnic.

Set_17.meaning: The group of people who make a country’s laws.
Set_17.example: Parliament debated the new education bill.

Set_17.meaning: Hard work; workers as a group.
Set_17.example: Building the wall took a lot of labour.

Set_17.meaning: The highest point of a hill or mountain; a high‑level meeting.
Set_17.example: They reached the summit by midday.

Set_17.meaning: Attractively old‑fashioned.
Set_17.example: They stayed in a quaint little cottage.

Set_17.meaning: Very careful about small details.
Set_17.example: Her notes were neat and meticulous.

Set_17.meaning: Not knowing important facts or information.
Set_17.example: It is ignorant to judge without knowing the facts.

Set_17.meaning: To do something to help; to force someone to do something.
Set_17.example: I would be happy to oblige with your request.

Set_18.meaning: From one side to the other side of something.
Set_18.example: They ran across the field to the gate.

Set_18.meaning: A cut made into something, especially by a doctor.
Set_18.example: The surgeon made a small incision in the skin.

Set_18.meaning: A piece of writing or music; the way something is made up.
Set_18.example: She wrote a composition about her holiday.

Set_18.meaning: Not safe or suitable to eat.
Set_18.example: The berries looked pretty but were inedible.

Set_18.meaning: To say that someone has done something wrong.
Set_18.example: She did not want to accuse anyone without proof.

Set_18.meaning: A contest to see who is best.
Set_18.example: He won first prize in the maths competition.

Set_18.meaning: A mixture of two or more things.
Set_18.example: The cake was a combination of chocolate and orange.

Set_18.meaning: Completely; in every way.
Set_18.example: The mistake was entirely my fault.

Set_18.meaning: A reason or details that make something clear.
Set_18.example: His clear explanation helped everyone understand.

Set_18.meaning: More than is needed.
Set_18.example: Delete any superfluous words in your essay.

Set_18.meaning: A thing you can see or touch; to say you disagree.
Set_18.example: She did not object to the new rule.

Set_18.meaning: Using or giving a lot; rich and fancy.
Set_18.example: They gave her a lavish birthday party.

Set_18.meaning: Made longer or larger.
Set_18.example: They took an extended break over summer.

Set_18.meaning: An illness or serious problem.
Set_18.example: The doctor could not identify the malady.

Set_18.meaning: Thinking you are not better than others; simple.
Set_18.example: Despite his success, he remained humble.

Set_18.meaning: Allowing fresh air to move through a room.
Set_18.example: Good ventilation keeps the room fresh.

Set_18.meaning: Likely to change suddenly; can easily catch fire.
Set_18.example: The situation remained volatile.

Set_18.meaning: Completely.
Set_18.example: The decision was wholly unexpected.

Set_18.meaning: A spoken or written account of something.
Set_18.example: Write a description of your favourite place.

Set_18.meaning: The day after today.
Set_18.example: We will start the new topic tomorrow.

Set_18.meaning: A cruel and unfair ruler.
Set_18.example: The people rebelled against the tyrant.

Set_18.meaning: The edge of something; the space at the side of a page.
Set_18.example: Write your name in the margin of the paper.

Set_18.meaning: The natural world around us; the surroundings.
Set_18.example: We must protect the environment from pollution.

Set_18.meaning: Work or trade done to earn money; a company.
Set_18.example: Her parents run a small family business.

Set_18.meaning: Calm and peaceful.
Set_18.example: The lake was tranquil at dawn.

Set_18.meaning: Full of strong and conflicting emotions; stormy.
Set_18.example: They had a tempestuous argument.

Set_18.meaning: To dry up and die.
Set_18.example: Without water, plants will wither.

Set_18.meaning: Showing that you love or care about someone.
Set_18.example: The dog is very affectionate towards its owner.

Set_18.meaning: The words we use to speak or write.
Set_18.example: English is the language used in this test.

Set_18.meaning: To make someone believe or agree to something.
Set_18.example: He tried to convince his parents to let him go.

Set_19.meaning: A dark mark on the skin caused by a hit.
Set_19.example: He got a bruise on his leg after falling.

Set_19.meaning: Showy in a way that tries to impress.
Set_19.example: His ostentatious car drew everyone’s attention.

Set_19.meaning: A serious promise.
Set_19.example: He made a pledge to work harder.

Set_19.meaning: To announce something publicly and strongly.
Set_19.example: The king will proclaim a new holiday.

Set_19.meaning: Very unhappy or in a very bad state.
Set_19.example: She felt wretched after the argument.

Set_19.meaning: A very unpleasant and difficult experience.
Set_19.example: The long journey in the rain was an ordeal.

Set_19.meaning: Treatment using exercises and movement.
Set_19.example: She had physiotherapy after her injury.

Set_19.meaning: A situation with two opposite or very different things.
Set_19.example: There is a clear polarity between their views.

Set_19.meaning: A place people go to on holiday; to turn to something for help.
Set_19.example: They stayed at a seaside resort.

Set_19.meaning: Grand and impressive.
Set_19.example: The mountains looked majestic in the sunset.

Set_19.meaning: The second of two things mentioned; the later part.
Set_19.example: Of tea and coffee, I prefer the latter.

Set_19.meaning: Using more than is needed.
Set_19.example: Leaving lights on is wasteful.

Set_19.meaning: Careful thought; concern for other people’s feelings.
Set_19.example: He showed great consideration for his neighbours.

Set_19.meaning: Not easily seen or noticed.
Set_19.example: He wore inconspicuous clothes to blend in.

Set_19.meaning: A person who plans and creates dance moves.
Set_19.example: The choreographer taught the dancers a new routine.

Set_19.meaning: How much of something there is.
Set_19.example: She checked the amount of water in the bottle.

Set_19.meaning: The way a word is spoken.
Set_19.example: His pronunciation of “queue” was correct.

Set_19.meaning: Having a pleasant smell.
Set_19.example: The garden was full of fragrant roses.

Set_19.meaning: To keep something.
Set_19.example: It is hard to retain so many new words.

Set_19.meaning: A scientist who studies fossils.
Set_19.example: The palaeontologist discovered a new dinosaur bone.

Set_19.meaning: The part of your body between neck and arm.
Set_19.example: He carried the bag on his shoulder.

Set_19.meaning: Worried or fearful about something that might happen.
Set_19.example: He was apprehensive about starting his new school.

Set_19.meaning: To stop a fire or thirst.
Set_19.example: Water will quench your thirst.

Set_19.meaning: Usual and traditional; not new or different.
Set_19.example: She chose a conventional black dress for the event.

Set_19.meaning: To hold back from doing something.
Set_19.example: He had to restrain himself from shouting.
